Garlic (Allium sativum) is a perennial plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its strong-smelling bulbs and edible flowers. Garlic has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and possessing antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Garlic is commonly prepared as a crude extract, oil, or supplement, and is used in various herbal preparations, including capsules, tablets, and topical creams.
